title: A Fully Quantum Algorithm for Image Edge Detection
abstract: This work introduces a novel quantum algorithm for gradient-based edge detection that operates entirely within the quantum circuit model. Grayscale images are encoded using the Novel Enhanced Quantum Representation (NEQR), allowing exact arithmetic on pixel intensities. Directional gradients are computed by generating superpositions of neighboring pixels via cyclic shift operations and performing subtraction with an exact quantum arithmetic circuit. To refine accuracy, we introduce a direction-aware shifting mechanism that aligns edges with the darker side of intensity transitions. Our novel Quantum Partitioning Algorithm enables efficient in-place thresholding of edge candidates. This work exhibits polynomial-time improvements and optimizes the ancilla count compared to previous NEQR-based quantum edge detection algorithms. These results demonstrate a resource-efficient and fully quantum approach to edge detection, highlighting a practical quantum advantage in image processing.
summary: This paper introduces a new quantum algorithm for edge detection that operates entirely within a quantum circuit without requiring classical post-processing. By using the NEQR representation and a novel phase oracle for thresholding, the authors reduce the number of required ancilla qubits and improve runtime complexity. The approach includes a unique direction-aware shifting mechanism to improve edge localization accuracy. Ultimately, it demonstrates a practical quantum advantage for image processing tasks by achieving exponential compression of the output.
main_problem: The development of an efficient, scalable quantum algorithm for image edge detection that avoids the need for classical post-processing and reduces the high qubit/gate complexity of previous NEQR-based methods.
main_result: The paper presents a fully quantum algorithm that achieves exponential compression of the output edge map and polynomial-time improvements in both time and space complexity compared to existing quantum edge detection models.
method: The algorithm uses NEQR encoding for grayscale images, implements gradient computation via cyclic shifts and quantum arithmetic, and employs a novel Quantum Partitioning Algorithm with a Fast Threshold Phase Oracle (FTPO) for efficient thresholding.
model_or_system: The algorithm operates within the quantum circuit model using the Novel Enhanced Quantum Representation (NEQR) to encode grayscale pixel intensities deterministically in the computational basis.
key_observables: The magnitude of the intensity gradient (delta I) and the sign bit of the gradient.
important_parameters: Image resolution (2^n x 2^n), grayscale levels (2^q), and the intensity threshold (T).
main_assumptions: The efficiency of the thresholding module assumes that thresholds have a constant number of leading zeros, and the NEQR encoding step is assumed to have a manageable overhead.
figures_summary: Fig 1-2 show quantum arithmetic submodules (QRCA, MAJ, UMA); Fig 3-5 detail the algorithm pipeline, pixel neighborhood generation, and subtraction modules; Fig 6-8 illustrate direction-aware shifting and the FTPO implementation; Fig 9 shows experimental edge map results.
paper_structure: The paper identifies limitations in classical and prior quantum edge detection, introduces the NEQR-based pipeline (encoding, gradient, shifting, thresholding), provides detailed circuit constructions for arithmetic and partitioning, performs complexity analysis, and concludes with experimental validation.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
